formulas.name: Law of Superposition
formulas.notes: The principle that in any undisturbed sequence of rocks deposited in layers, the youngest layer is on top and the oldest on the bottom, each layer being younger than the one beneath it and older than the one above it.

formulas.name: Law of Original Horizontality
formulas.notes: The principle that the layers of rock are deposited in a horizontal position. This is the original position of the rock layers.

formulas.name: Principle of Inclusions
formulas.notes: The principle that any rock fragment or fossil found in one rock layer can only have come from a layer that is either above or below the layer in which it is found.

formulas.name: Principle of Faunal Succession
formulas.notes: The principle that the different types of fossil species found in rock layers can be used to date the layers.

formulas.name: Principle of Cross-Cutting Relationships
formulas.notes: The principle that a rock or feature that cuts across other rocks is younger than the rocks it cuts across.

formulas.name: Principle of Unconformity
formulas.notes: The principle that in any sequence of rocks, there can be gaps in the record where rock layers have been eroded away before being covered by younger layers.
